We have 60

So I've already kicked that off on our side, you know, again, if the content of the article doesn't change dramatically, we already have the kind of like CMS structure, so we're kind of building off of that, and then any small tweaks will adapt as they come.
Yeah.
Yeah.
Yeah.

there's step there's a companies You

And then we'll probably need to file a ticket for our reverse proxy to get that pointed to slash integrations.
Got it.
Are you taking, just wanted to quickly.
So, quickly, were you taking the lead on this?

Because Colin was saying probably you were already working with a web team on this one.

Okay.

Yeah, I ran it.
I trained a GPT with the messaging doc that Michael sent over, and then ran it through that.
Cool.
I can review and give comments on this today.
I did not know you guys retrained that fast.
Cool.
That's great.

Yeah, I can touch base with the web team today about that.
Okay, sounds good.
Yeah, so there's articles here.
I'll show some of stuff at the end.
kind of ping me around LLM tracking.
We're doing that for you now.

Yep.

I'll make sure you're added so you can review both.

I think this one seems less urgent because I guess one of the unknowns from our...

I don't know if there's an answer for that or if this is something you want us to support as well.
Because I guess similar to the integration pages, we'd also be happy to just build something and then you point a reverse proxy for it.

Bettennations?
Bettennations.

Yeah, that was my, that was my developer hub pitch, right?

then, I think last week, Rachel joined, I think, I mean, directionally still aligned, but it seemed like she was talking about maybe having like a separate home.
So I'm not sure at the moment what the plan is there.
Are we still moving towards like a subdirectory within the blog?
Yeah, so, yeah, okay, Karab, you can, let me, because we've kind of discussed this multiple times.
I think, Jason, right now, let's let's unlock you.
And Michael, Vivian, you guys should like correct me if I'm wrong.

Yeah.
Cool.
Colin, make sense?

Yep.

We'll just send a message with all the articles, Colin.
sounds like you're the reviewer for now.
I'm the reviewer for now.
Luke is going to come back into the fray for a bit.
Well, hopefully for a longer bit, and we will be tag-teaming on this.
Okay.
Okay.
That's good.
Okay, I think, yeah, so that's kind of our update here.
I do want to show some stuff around prompt monitoring, but before that, any questions for the three kind of categories of content here?
Okay, I think that's pretty clear.
Great, so let's look at prompts.
So we sent over around like 70-ish prompts, which we're now tracking across ChatGPT and Perplexity.
I just want to show this.
I'm working on giving you guys access to a dashboard, whether it's this one or because we're kind of getting data from multiple sources.
So we're stitching that together and give you like a look or something like that.
information, for함.
I am working on that.

Basically, so far, we have 70 prompts, all in the realm, more on the developer side, where we are tracking kind of, is Webflow being mentioned, what's driving the answer to a prompt, and, you know, what's the sentiment when you are mentioned, and also, if your competitors are being mentioned.
And, so, I mean, just one on the monitoring side, just want to show how this all works.
So, you know, we've chosen a bunch of prompts.
Again, there's no kind of definitive best practice there at the moment.
It's not like keywords where you know the volume, you know everyone's searching for it.
It's a little bit.
of a guessing game based on what you know about your customers, how they find your product.
Also using search keywords as a proxy.
So, you know, that's how these prompts are defined, or how we come up with them.
you know, and then...
What we're tracking is, you know, when you prompt for that particular set of words, what the response is.
And in that response, when there are citations, what are the citations?
So in this example, you know, we're asking best CMS alternatives for WordPress for developers.
You know, the answer is have Webflow in it, which is great.
And you can see the citations are, for the most part, listicles, almost entirely listicles.
Let's see.
Yeah.
So, you know, one, we're tracking this.
And the V1 of how this is affecting kind of our work is we want to see if you have content like this.
If you don't, we'll add it to the backlog.
If you do, we'll take a look at why it's either.
You're not being, not ranking or not being cited and come up with ways to help you.
Fresh, the content so that it is cited.
right now we have, you know, 70 prompts.
We'll probably have more over time.
I think willing, like we can probably track up to 200.
Anything over that, I mean, I'm we should talk about.
But for Webflow, I mean, we're willing to track, honestly, as many as you guys can think of.
And yeah, it's basically shifting, shaping our content backlog.
Any questions?
Is there a way to aggregate all of those citation sources?
Mm-hmm.
I just have it right here.
You can see, again, this is heavily kind of shaped by what prompts you decide to track.
But at least in the kind of developer category of trying to pick a CMS, I mean, patterns we've seen is there's a lot of listicles, understandably.
And a lot of variants of listicles that if you only looked at search keywords, you might not write like a separate.
Article for each one or try to even target them.
But for LLM tracking, I mean, if you believe in the prompts you've chosen, we've seen just like a little bit more long tail stuff, you know, than usual.
Yeah, so I can share it.
mean, I'll share all this with you as well, because I think a lot of this falls in the domain of like stuff you write for the blog that we haven't necessarily been scoped to help out with.
That's what I'm saying.
Like, if you have other prompts you want to track, just like throw it in there and it can shape, I don't know, I guess the other stuff you publish on the blog as well.
Okay, yeah, we have like a list of other prompts we're tracking with Graphite, but let me pull those and see if we can maybe add it here or just like compare.
Yeah, sounds good.
and then.
I'm trying to think what else.

Yeah, I mean, some of the stuff that we're trying to still get a better grasp on is we see a lot of cases of, you know, if you look at the search results, the kind of search query, like these are not positions one through four.
Like they're somewhere usually within the first like three to five pages.
Um, and part of what we're trying to understand more deeply, you know, is like what really shapes certain article getting.
Cause sometimes like when you look at them, they're not great articles when you look at them, you know, they're very thin.
Um, I this one's just kind of like a pretty UI heavy page.
Um, anyway, but over time, we're trying to do is help you guys get, or help you essentially shape, you know, the answers to the prompts.
And also get cited.
Um, a lot of those articles are also like pay to play type of articles.
Yeah.
So if you guys do link building already.
I think that's helpful for those in that team.
We also see a lot of stuff that ends up on Reddit or Quora.
mean, Reddit's probably the most frequently seen site in citations.
And I don't know if you guys have a community team with a strategy there, but that is something we help people with as well.
Like we'll repurpose certain topics for Reddit and then figure out what subreddits and communities.
It make sense to post.
Yeah, but as a start, I mean, we're going to monitor or send over the other prompts.
monitor those as well.
I think another category of prompts we've seen people track, which I think is important, is like more on the valuation side.
Like somebody asked like, hey, does Webflow support this?
You know, does Webflow integrate with this or that?
I mean, I guess integrations we should come up with because we're doing the integrations.
But I think a lot of this leads into like you.
Product marketing team and how they think about positioning and messaging and if, you know, the albums are saying the right thing.
So if you have prompts there, we can track those as well.
Does Scrunch generate prompts for you or is it all based on like...
They do, but generally I almost overwrite all of them because they're usually not great.
And we actually talked to the Scrunch founder and there's no magic behind how they're generating those prompts.
It's kind of just like a way to give you a few so their app doesn't look empty.
I think over time, I guess somebody is going to try to figure out how to correlate, you know, everything together to like actually give you some estimates on volume.
But right now, at least I haven't seen any tools that offer that.
Um, yeah.
A reason you asked for that?
Like, do you guys need help with generating?
No, I was just comparing different, like, LLM tracking tools, and a lot of them are either, you know, like you mentioned, generate the prompts, or you have to input them yourself.
Yeah.
Yep.
Cool.
So I think I'll stop there.
Lots of action items.
Let's see.
I mean, our goal is really just getting the integration pages live.
So we're working on that.
And, yeah.
Any other questions?
We could probably end early.
Like, I will say, maybe on the product marketing side, or the talk about Webflow side, like the page you shared, like, is that something that's, I guess, changing at all?
Or that's pretty much set?
Because I think one of the things we noticed is, like...
I think that is the term.
It is changing?
It is changing.
Okay.
Part of Luke's remit is also restructuring our developer, our entire narrative and positioning.
So there is active work in reframing those.
Okay.
That's helpful.
Because one of the things, I mean, I guess, first, how you talk about your product and all your content seems to be really important, especially for LLM.
Citations.
We've seen, like, for the most part, what you say in that one article is what shapes whether ChatGPT says this or that about you, not like they won't look at your homepage.
There's some trained-in knowledge, but it usually gets overrided.

So something that's really helpful that we're trying to add is once you have a very defined kind of framework for messaging, we can essentially crawl your site, look at blog posts you published through.
There's a go, forum posts that might be shaping random LLM kind of answers and just flag that for you.
And then for certain things, we'll be able to just kind of automatically change it for you as well.
So whenever you guys have that guideline, we can try to set something like that up.
Andrew should be working on that.
And he's actually, he's been playing around with adjustments to prompt engineering to help get more aligned in terms of our article structures.
So I hope this week, Luke, you and I can probably connect and talk about that.
We do want to help get the framing right.
And that's the thing we're actively on our side trying to plan out.
Cool.
Sounds good.
And the way I look at it is like, you might publish some stuff and changing it later, like, it's not a big deal.
So.
So.
Cool.
I think that's it for me.
Talk to you all soon.
We'll update you async and slack, but we have our marching orders and we're here to move quickly.
Cool.
All right.
Thank you all for your time.
Talk to you all later.
Right on.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Sync on content creation progress and strategies for Webflow's integration pages, regular blog, and developer blog.](https://fathom.video/share/aGEteYL4xpKJMBSrCCrky_6x7-_Xexcs?tab=summary&timestamp=136.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [Integration pages: 60 created, 60-100 more by week's end; building in Webflow, awaiting project setup](https://fathom.video/share/aGEteYL4xpKJMBSrCCrky_6x7-_Xexcs?tab=summary&timestamp=168.0)
  - [Regular blog: 5-6 calibration articles written, adapting to new Webflow messaging guidelines](https://fathom.video/share/aGEteYL4xpKJMBSrCCrky_6x7-_Xexcs?tab=summary&timestamp=407.0)
  - [Developer blog: Content initially to be posted on main blog (2-3/week) to gauge performance before potential separate blog](https://fathom.video/share/aGEteYL4xpKJMBSrCCrky_6x7-_Xexcs?tab=summary&timestamp=684.0)
  - [LLM prompt tracking: 70+ prompts monitored across ChatGPT/Perplexity, shaping content strategy](https://fathom.video/share/aGEteYL4xpKJMBSrCCrky_6x7-_Xexcs?tab=summary&timestamp=866.0)

## Topics

### Integration Pages Progress

  - [60 pages created, reformatting based on last week's suggestions](https://fathom.video/share/aGEteYL4xpKJMBSrCCrky_6x7-_Xexcs?tab=summary&timestamp=172.0)
  - [60-100 more pages to be generated by end of week](https://fathom.video/share/aGEteYL4xpKJMBSrCCrky_6x7-_Xexcs?tab=summary&timestamp=179.0)
  - [Building in Webflow, awaiting project setup and reverse proxy for /integrations](https://fathom.video/share/aGEteYL4xpKJMBSrCCrky_6x7-_Xexcs?tab=summary&timestamp=195.0)
  - [Team can adapt to small design tweaks as they come](https://fathom.video/share/aGEteYL4xpKJMBSrCCrky_6x7-_Xexcs?tab=summary&timestamp=365.0)

### Regular Blog Content

  - [5-6 calibration articles written, incorporating new Webflow messaging guidelines](https://fathom.video/share/aGEteYL4xpKJMBSrCCrky_6x7-_Xexcs?tab=summary&timestamp=414.0)
  - [Sydney added framing for Headless CMS and Composable CMS](https://fathom.video/share/aGEteYL4xpKJMBSrCCrky_6x7-_Xexcs?tab=summary&timestamp=473.0)
  - [Colin to review and provide comments on new content](https://fathom.video/share/aGEteYL4xpKJMBSrCCrky_6x7-_Xexcs?tab=summary&timestamp=519.0)
  - [Awaiting cloned blog project from Webflow team for content staging](https://fathom.video/share/aGEteYL4xpKJMBSrCCrky_6x7-_Xexcs?tab=summary&timestamp=540.0)

### Developer Blog Strategy

  - [Initially posting technical articles (2-3/week) on main blog to assess performance](https://fathom.video/share/aGEteYL4xpKJMBSrCCrky_6x7-_Xexcs?tab=summary&timestamp=769.0)
  - [Potential for separate developer blog in future](https://fathom.video/share/aGEteYL4xpKJMBSrCCrky_6x7-_Xexcs?tab=summary&timestamp=758.0)
  - [Colin and Luke to review developer content](https://fathom.video/share/aGEteYL4xpKJMBSrCCrky_6x7-_Xexcs?tab=summary&timestamp=744.0)
  - [Keyword mapping and cluster mapping done for potential content hubs](https://fathom.video/share/aGEteYL4xpKJMBSrCCrky_6x7-_Xexcs?tab=summary&timestamp=638.0)

### LLM Prompt Tracking

  - [70+ prompts tracked across ChatGPT and Perplexity](https://fathom.video/share/aGEteYL4xpKJMBSrCCrky_6x7-_Xexcs?tab=summary&timestamp=866.0)
  - [Monitoring Webflow mentions, driving factors, sentiment, and competitor mentions](https://fathom.video/share/aGEteYL4xpKJMBSrCCrky_6x7-_Xexcs?tab=summary&timestamp=901.0)
  - [Shaping content backlog based on tracking results](https://fathom.video/share/aGEteYL4xpKJMBSrCCrky_6x7-_Xexcs?tab=summary&timestamp=998.0)
  - [Aggregated citation sources reveal patterns (e.g., listicles, Reddit, Quora)](https://fathom.video/share/aGEteYL4xpKJMBSrCCrky_6x7-_Xexcs?tab=summary&timestamp=1204.0)
  - [Potential to track up to 200 prompts; team open to tracking more as needed](https://fathom.video/share/aGEteYL4xpKJMBSrCCrky_6x7-_Xexcs?tab=summary&timestamp=1029.0)

### Webflow Messaging and Positioning

  - [Current messaging guidelines are in flux](https://fathom.video/share/aGEteYL4xpKJMBSrCCrky_6x7-_Xexcs?tab=summary&timestamp=1362.0)
  - [Luke working on restructuring developer narrative and positioning](https://fathom.video/share/aGEteYL4xpKJMBSrCCrky_6x7-_Xexcs?tab=summary&timestamp=1385.0)
  - [Team to set up system to flag and update old content once new guidelines are established](https://fathom.video/share/aGEteYL4xpKJMBSrCCrky_6x7-_Xexcs?tab=summary&timestamp=1397.0)

## Next Steps

  - [Kirat to lead integration pages project setup with web team](https://fathom.video/share/aGEteYL4xpKJMBSrCCrky_6x7-_Xexcs?tab=summary&timestamp=244.0)
  - [Vivian to touch base with web team about cloning blog project](https://fathom.video/share/aGEteYL4xpKJMBSrCCrky_6x7-_Xexcs?tab=summary&timestamp=551.0)
  - [Colin to review and comment on new blog content](https://fathom.video/share/aGEteYL4xpKJMBSrCCrky_6x7-_Xexcs?tab=summary&timestamp=519.0)
  - [Jason to ensure Colin has access to integration page layouts](https://fathom.video/share/aGEteYL4xpKJMBSrCCrky_6x7-_Xexcs?tab=summary&timestamp=391.0)
  - [Vivian to share additional prompts tracked with Graphite for comparison](https://fathom.video/share/aGEteYL4xpKJMBSrCCrky_6x7-_Xexcs?tab=summary&timestamp=1324.0)
  - [Luke and Colin to connect on prompt engineering and article structure adjustments](https://fathom.video/share/aGEteYL4xpKJMBSrCCrky_6x7-_Xexcs?tab=summary&timestamp=1464.0)
  - [GrowthX team to continue content creation and await further instructions on messaging guidelines](https://fathom.video/share/aGEteYL4xpKJMBSrCCrky_6x7-_Xexcs?tab=summary&timestamp=1507.0)

action_items.description: Create new Webflow project for integrations pages; invite Jason's team
File ticket for reverse proxy to point /integrations to new project
Ensure Colin has access to review integrations content page layout
Review Headless e-commerce platform article; provide comments
Touch base w/ web team re cloning blog project for GrowthX; provide workspace ID
Create hub page for finding/searching integration content
Pull Graphite prompt list; compare/add to Jason's LLM tracking
